questions.question: Twin studies in behavioral genetics suggest that personality traits are approximately:

options.text: 0% heritable because personality is entirely learned

questions.explanation: Research using twin studies consistently shows that major personality traits are moderately heritable, with genetic factors accounting for roughly 40-60% of the variation, while the remaining variation is attributable to environmental factors.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes temperament?

options.text: Personality traits acquired through social learning during adolescence
Innate, biologically-based tendencies in emotional reactivity and self-regulation that appear early in life
Unconscious conflicts that emerge during Freud's psychosexual stages
The set of defense mechanisms a person relies on most frequently

questions.explanation: Temperament refers to innate, biologically-based tendencies that are observable from the earliest weeks of life and remain relatively stable, providing a biological foundation for later personality development.
